headline: Russia FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — day-3 final close of US-brokered trilateral ceasefire (May 9-11) completes the cumulative 6-event ceasefire-breach record. No score change (floor). Day-3 conduct documentation: (1) Russian drones, bombs and artillery struck Kharkiv and Kherson civilian areas, killing at least 2 (incl. 14-year-old wounded among 7) and wounding 7+. 38 Russian attacks reported by 16:00 (Ukrainian General Staff). (2) Russian MoD continued 'strict observance' claim while strikes proceeded — bad-faith-INT pattern documented across ALL THREE ceasefire days without exception. (3) Prisoner exchange blame-shifting continued (Putin: 'Ukraine not ready'); exchange NOT completed by ceasefire expiry. (4) ANALYTICALLY SIGNIFICANT DAY-3 ADDITION — ISW retrospective finding (May 12 publication, in-window analysis): Russia used the Victory Day ceasefire as a STRATEGIC STOCKPILE WINDOW. Drone strikes rose during the truce (pre-positioned Molniya drones deployed); reserves repositioned forward in Lyman direction. This converts bad-faith conduct from individual-breach-level to strategic-format-exploitation level — Russia did not merely violate the ceasefire while continuing operations, Russia treated the ceasefire structure as a tactical opportunity to consolidate offensive capability. Cumulative 6-event breach record (3 unilateral collapses May 5-7 + 3 trilateral breaches May 9-10-11) is now COMPLETE. Floor sustained; conduct documentation appended.
rotation_state_data_integrity_flag: Rotation-state.json shows Russia composite 6.3; canonical countries.json shows 0.0 (all dimensions at 1, floor). Per hard constraint, canonical value used (0.0). Same staleness class flagged on May 8, 9, 10. Rotation-state sync action remains required separately from this proposal.

conduct_documentation.summary: Day-3 final close of trilateral ceasefire window: breach pattern sustained AND analytically deepened by ISW strategic-stockpile-window finding. 6-event cumulative breach record (3 unilateral + 3 trilateral) is now complete.
new_conduct_categories_this_cycle: Day 3 trilateral-ceasefire breach: Russian drones, bombs and artillery struck Kharkiv and Kherson civilian areas, killing at least 2 (one Kherson resident; one in Kharkiv) and wounding 7+ including a 14-year-old boy. 38 Russian attacks by 16:00 per Ukrainian General Staff.

Sustained bad-faith-INT pattern (third consecutive trilateral day): Russian MoD continued 'strict observance' framing while strikes proceeded — false-observance claim documented across full 3-day window without exception.

Prisoner exchange completion failure: Exchange did not complete by ceasefire expiry. Putin maintained 'Ukraine not ready' blame-shift on day 3; Ukraine publicly rebutted with list-submission record. Russia's posture on the exchange is documented as non-execution rather than substantive cooperation.

STRATEGIC-LEVEL BAD-FAITH ADDITION (ISW retrospective, in-window analysis): Russia used the Victory Day ceasefire as a STRATEGIC STOCKPILE WINDOW. Pre-positioned Molniya drones deployed, drone strikes ROSE during truce, reserves repositioned forward in Lyman direction. This is qualitatively distinct from individual-strike-level breaches: Russia structurally exploited the ceasefire format to consolidate offensive capability. The finding registers as a new strategic-format-exploitation conduct category in the floor record.
cumulative_ceasefire_breach_record_windo: May 5: Unilateral ceasefire declaration, immediate collapse with documented Russian strikes (per May 7 rule cycle)
May 6: Second unilateral declaration, immediate collapse (per May 7 rule cycle)
May 7: Third unilateral declaration, immediate collapse (per May 7 rule cycle)
May 9 (trilateral day 1): Kharkiv residential building drone strike — 5 injured including two 8-year-old boys; MoD claimed 'strict observance' simultaneously
May 10 (trilateral day 2): 3 civilians killed, 19+ injured across Kharkiv/Kherson; ~210 battlefield clashes; MoD claimed 16,071 alleged Ukrainian violations; Putin blame-shifted prisoner exchange
May 11 (trilateral day 3): 2+ killed, 7 wounded incl. 14-year-old; 38 Russian attacks by 16:00; prisoner exchange not completed; Putin sustained blame-shift; MoD continued 'strict observance' framing

STRATEGIC OVERLAY (ISW May 12, analyzes May 9-11): Russia used the ceasefire as a strategic stockpile window — strikes rose during truce; Molniya drones pre-positioned and deployed; reserves repositioned forward in Lyman direction. Strategic-format-exploitation conduct documented across the full 3-day trilateral period.

strategic_format_exploitation_classifica.finding: Per ISW, Russia did not merely violate the ceasefire while continuing operations — Russia STRUCTURALLY EXPLOITED THE CEASEFIRE FORMAT. The reduced large-scale strategic bombardment during the truce was not a sign of partial compliance; it was a deliberate tactical pause to redeploy Molniya drones and reposition reserves. Drone strikes actually ROSE during the ceasefire window. Russia treated the trilateral ceasefire structure as a tactical opportunity rather than a humanitarian pause.
strategic_format_exploitation_classifica.implication_for_floor_record: The strategic-format-exploitation conduct is qualitatively distinct from individual-strike breaches. It documents that Russia engages with ceasefire structures in bad faith as a category, not just on individual days. This is a structural rather than incident-level finding and significantly raises the evidentiary burden for any future Russian good-faith ceasefire claim.
strategic_format_exploitation_classifica.implication_for_int_dimension: INT floor is sustained AND analytically deepened. Russia's stated commitment to ceasefire structures is now documented as instrumentalized — the declared posture (ceasefire observance) is contradicted not just by individual breaches but by the strategic structure of the underlying conduct.

conduct_documentation.floor_exit_criteria_impact: Six-event cumulative ceasefire-breach record across a seven-day window (May 5-11) + strategic-format-exploitation finding provides an exceptionally dense evidentiary basis for floor-conduct documentation. Floor-exit criteria would require Russia to demonstrate sustained good-faith conduct in any future ceasefire context. The strategic-exploitation finding raises the evidentiary burden further: floor exit would require not just incident-level compliance but a demonstrably non-instrumental engagement with the ceasefire structure itself.

key_evidence: May 11 2026 (in-window, day 3 ceasefire close): Russian drones, bombs and artillery struck Kharkiv and Kherson civilian areas, killing at least 2 and wounding 7+ including a 14-year-old boy (PBS, Washington Times). 38 Russian attacks reported by 16:00 (Ukrainian General Staff).
May 11 2026 (in-window): Russian MoD continued 'strict observance' framing while strikes proceeded — bad-faith-INT pattern documented across the full 3-day trilateral window without exception.

May 11 2026 (in-window): Putin sustained 'Ukraine not ready' blame-shift on prisoner exchange (Euromaidan Press); Ukraine rebutted with public list-submission record. Russia did not complete the exchange by ceasefire expiry.

May 12 2026 (just outside window; analyzes in-window conduct; permitted per prompt's hard constraint): ISW retrospective finding — Russia used the Victory Day ceasefire as a STRATEGIC STOCKPILE WINDOW. Drone strikes ROSE during the truce; Molniya drones pre-positioned and deployed; reserves repositioned forward in Lyman direction (Euromaidan Press citing ISW).

Cumulative 6-event ceasefire-breach record (May 5-11): 3 unilateral collapses (May 5-7) + 3 trilateral breaches (May 9-10-11). This is the most dense ceasefire-breach record documented for any single entity in the active rotation queue.

STRATEGIC-FORMAT-EXPLOITATION CONDUCT CATEGORY ADDED: Per ISW, Russia structurally exploited the ceasefire format rather than merely violating individual provisions. New floor-record conduct category logged.

evidence.finding: Day-3 Russian strikes killed at least 2 civilians and wounded 7+ (incl. 14-year-old boy) in Kharkiv/Kherson. Ceasefire expired with fighting persisting and prisoner exchange incomplete.

evidence.finding: Day-3 prisoner exchange remained in coordination phase; Russia maintained blame-shift framing; exchange not completed by ceasefire expiry.

evidence.source: Euromaidan Press — Moscow's Victory Day ceasefire reduced combat but became Russia's stockpile window, ISW finds
evidence.finding: ISW retrospective (in-window analysis): Russia used the ceasefire as a STRATEGIC STOCKPILE WINDOW. Drone strikes ROSE during truce; pre-positioned Molniya drones deployed; reserves repositioned forward in Lyman direction. Strategic-format-exploitation conduct documented.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1 unchanged (floor). Suppression of independent monitoring sustained; controlled-narrative posture continues. Day-3 false-observance framing documented.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1 unchanged (floor). Continued targeting of Ukrainian civilian residential areas (Kharkiv, Kherson) on day 3 — at least 2 killed, 7 wounded including a 14-year-old boy.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 unchanged (floor). Prisoner exchange not completed at expiry; sustained front-line offensive operations; harm-causing action not reduced.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 unchanged (floor). No EQ-relevant changes in window; structural pattern sustained.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 unchanged (floor). Ceasefire-claim vs. action contradiction sustained across all 3 trilateral days = B3 scope-clarity floor active. STRATEGIC FORMAT EXPLOITATION (ISW) is a structural B-dimension violation: cooperative-framework participation is instrumentalized rather than genuine.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1 unchanged (floor). Defense Ministry false-observance claims simultaneous with documented violations across all 3 days — AB1 harm-acknowledgment floor active; AB2 transparency floor active.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1 unchanged (floor). No new SYS conduct category this cycle. Floor sustained.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1 unchanged (floor). Declared trilateral ceasefire vs. 3-day documented breach pattern + blame-shifting on prisoner exchange + STRATEGIC FORMAT EXPLOITATION (ISW) = bad-faith-INT pattern documented at strategic level, not just incident level. Sixth confirmation cycle of bad-faith-ceasefire rule (May 5, 6, 7, 9, 10, 11). Strategic-format-exploitation conduct is a qualitatively distinct INT floor anchor: Russia engages with ceasefire structures in bad faith as a category, not just on individual days.

math_check.validator_check: Floor sustained. Composite cannot move below floor. New conduct documented for floor record: strategic-format-exploitation category added.

trilateral_methodology_call_final: FINAL TRILATERAL ATTRIBUTION RULE COMPLETE ACROSS DAYS 1-2-3. Russia is the documented counterparty in the bad-faith-ceasefire rule applied on May 5, May 7, May 9, May 10, and May 11. The full 3-day trilateral conduct record + ISW strategic-stockpile-window finding is now complete. For floor-designated entities, partial compliance does not generate composite movement (floor cannot move). The trilateral methodology call (evidence-by-evidence attribution + strategic-format-exploitation overlay) registers Russia's full-window breaches at both incident and strategic levels. Cumulative 6-event breach record + strategic-format-exploitation conduct category provides the densest floor-conduct evidentiary basis for any entity in the active rotation.
negativeSignals: Day-3 ceasefire breach with civilian casualties (incl. 14-year-old); sustained false-observance framing across all 3 trilateral days; prisoner exchange not completed; sustained blame-shifting; cumulative 6-event breach record; STRATEGIC-FORMAT-EXPLOITATION CONDUCT (ISW): drone strikes rose during truce, Molniya drones pre-positioned, reserves repositioned forward in Lyman direction. Russia structurally exploited the ceasefire format rather than merely violating it.
positiveSignals: Large-scale strategic bombardment reduced during the truce — per ISW retrospective, this reduction was NOT good-faith partial compliance but a deliberate tactical pause to redeploy capability. The reduction is reclassified as part of the strategic-format-exploitation conduct, not as a positive signal. NO positive signals in window.
sector_context: Eastern Europe / active-conflict cluster. Russia's floor placement is reinforced by the sixth confirmation cycle of the bad-faith-ceasefire rule + the first documentation of strategic-format-exploitation conduct. Cross-reference: Ukraine (paired counterparty, 50.0 functional confirmed at day-3 close; sub-threshold dimensional upward pressure carried forward on ACT and INT).
